content:
- Ran the required repository gates after the new README-only novice contract slices: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed. Default suite stayed green; `make test-long` remained intentionally not applicable because long-lane selection was unchanged.
- Updated the story-24 task file to `<status>done</status>` and `<passes>true</passes>`, checked off the acceptance criteria with the long-lane note, and ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- Next: stage all modified files including `.ralph`, commit with the required `task finished ...` message including the passing checks, push, and quit immediately.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and .ralph/current_tasks_done.md, plus the active task files.
- Confirmed the actual repo stack is a Rust workspace (Cargo.toml and crates/*) with a vendored Go module under cockroachdb_molt and GitHub workflow work in scope.
- Found no active failing bug tasks in current_tasks.md. Active failing tasks are story-21 task 07 (suspended), story-23 task 01, and story-24 tasks 02 and 03.
- Resolved blockers from current_tasks_done.md: story-24 task 01 already passes; no active candidate had an unresolved <blocked_by> tag. Story-21 task 07 is suspended by its own task definition and should not be selected ahead of active non-suspended work.
- Most logical next task is .ralph/tasks/story-24-readme-only-novice-e2e/02-task-verify-readme-stays-short-user-facing-and-inline-config-driven.md because story-24 task 01 just completed, task 02 continues the same verification chain, and task 03 depends on the README/operator path task 02 validates.
- Next immediate action after quitting: keep story 24 moving in order, starting with task 02.

- Read previous progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill files.
- Verified the current task still points at story 24 task 02; previous progress says checks already passed and task switching already ran, so I am validating task file state and repo status before deciding whether only final closeout remains.
- Next: inspect the task markdown, confirm whether `<passes>true</passes>` and completion boxes are present, then commit/push if the repo is otherwise ready.

- Read the task-02 markdown, the related story-24/task-01 plan, the current README, and the existing novice/README test support to determine the right planning boundary.
- Found that task 02 had no plan yet and that README-content verification is currently mixed into `novice_registry_only_contract.rs` and `readme_public_image_workspace.rs`; also noted the current README still contains likely operator-noise sections such as contributor workflow, licensing, and CI publish safety.
- Added `.ralph/tasks/story-24-readme-only-novice-e2e/02-task-verify-readme-stays-short-user-facing-and-inline-config-driven_plans/2026-04-20-readme-operator-surface-plan.md` with `NOW EXECUTE`, and updated the task markdown with its `<plan>` pointer.
- Next: in the execution turn, follow the plan with strict red/green TDD by introducing a dedicated README operator-surface contract, pruning/moving non-operator README content, cleaning the test boundaries, then running `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` before marking the task passed.

- Read previous progress, the current task markdown, and the linked plan; confirmed the task plan ends with NOW EXECUTE and is not a planning-only turn.
- Observed a mismatch between prior progress (which says implementation/checks already passed and task switch already ran) and current git status (which currently shows only .ralph/task-state files as modified/untracked).
- Next: inspect git history and the task diff to determine whether the README/test changes are already committed and this turn only needs final closeout, or whether execution work still needs to be re-done.

- Added a dedicated `readme_operator_surface_contract.rs` test and got the first RED failure against the existing README, confirming the document still mixed operator guidance with maintainer-only sections.
- Rewrote `README.md` into three operator-only sections, added `readme_operator_surface.rs` as the new README boundary owner, switched the novice harness to that owner, and removed the old `readme_public_image_workspace.rs` support file.
- Next: add the remaining README-content behavior tests (ordering, inline artifacts, required/optional args), then run the runner test targets and the repository gates.
